rationale: RED-on-revert lens, empirically verified in a throwaway git worktree @8b664359 (original worktree left clean). All 11 named tests + the AC4 anchor pass at HEAD and map EXACTLY to the spec's named ACs (AC1a/1b/2/2d/3/4/5/6/6e/7b/11/13). Mutation 1 (neutralize the runOrphanObligationGate call) turns RED: OrphanRefuses, OrphanInfraErrorFailsClosed (all 3 legs), WorktreeEnumRefusesDespiteProbeMiss (race + list-error), ObligationBackstop, CorruptPlanRefuses (missing + no-bead_ids), HatchDoesNotBypassOrphan, AdvisorySlotNaming, and TestApproveImplCallOrder (anchor pos 0 -> not-found). These are genuinely RED-on-revert, not tautologies. The two PASS-on-gate-disable tests are correctly the opposite-direction pins and BOTH discriminate: AC2d DeletedBranchNoRefusal goes RED under an over-refusing Leg-1 mutant (proves it is not a hollow tautology); AC11 UnreadableRefStoreAbortsPreScan goes RED (scanCalled=true) when the pre-scan MergeBase fail-closed is neutralized — pinning the store-touch precondition and gate ordering. grep 'ScanOrphanedClosedBeads' impl_test.go = 2 (succeeds). Every priority check confirmed.

findings.detail: Refusal names bead-x/bead/bead-x/spec/010-test, final line 'recovery: mindspec complete bead-x', phaseWrites==0, epic-close closed==none, FinalizeEpic 0 calls. RED under gate-disable (t.Fatal 'expected an orphan refusal').

All three infra-error subcases (epic-lookup, bd-list, ancestry) refuse fail-closed with recovery line, phaseWrites==0, FinalizeEpic uncalled. All three go RED under gate-disable (flow reaches approveOKMock FinalizeEpic, err==nil).

Anti-false-refusal pin: scan nil + worktree nil -> proceeds to FinalizeEpic (called once). NOT a tautology — verified RED under Mutant A (Leg 1 refuses on empty scan): 'a genuinely-deleted branch must not refuse'. Correctly PASSES on gate-disable because it guards the opposite (over-refusal) direction.

MergeBase forced exit-128 aborts ApproveImpl before the orphan scan: scanCalled==false, phaseWrites==0, closed==none, FinalizeEpic uncalled. Discriminates — Mutant B (swallow the mbErr return) makes scanCalled==true -> RED at orphan_gate_test.go:611. Pins Fact 1 store-touch precondition + gate ordering after impl.go MergeBase.

Subcase (a) probe-miss + enumerated non-ancestor worktree refuses (names bead-1/bead/bead-1 + recovery line); (b) worktree-list error fails closed; (c) ancestor bead-1 + other-spec bead do NOT trigger (t.Fatalf guards ancestry-scope + FinalizeEpic once). (a)/(b) RED under gate-disable; (c) scoping enforced by fatal guards. Leg never consults branchExistsFn — keyed off implWorktreeListFn/implClosedEpicBeadIDsFn/implIsAncestorFn.

runOrphanObligationGate anchor sits between CheckADRDivergence and the mindspec_phase-only reconcile write, before close/done/CommitCount/FinalizeEpic (strict source-pos ordering). RED under gate-disable (anchor pos==0 -> 'anchor not found' FailNow). grep -c 'ScanOrphanedClosedBeads' impl_test.go = 2 (label + comment) -> grep -q succeeds.
MINDSPEC_SKIP_PANEL=1 and enforcement.panel_gate:false both still refuse; message never contains the skip-var string. RED under gate-disable.

Readable panel names the unresolved codex-correctness slot; removed panel -> identical refusal minus slot line; no 'refut' substring, no skip-var leak. All three RED under gate-disable (no refusal to decorate).

branch-less uncovered obligation -> restoration-prerequisite recourse before bare complete; covered -> proceeds (FinalizeEpic once); metadata read-error -> fail-closed bare-command recourse; hatch does not bypass. Refusal subcases RED under gate-disable.

Missing plan.md and valid-frontmatter-without-bead_ids both refuse naming 'plan bead list could not be read', FinalizeEpic uncalled — Leg 3 fail-closed on planErr, distinct from gate-1/3 silent skip. Both RED under gate-disable.
Post-settle ancestor state (scan nil, worktree bead-1 ancestor) passes the gate and reaches FinalizeEpic once — convergence pin, consistent with the clean path.
Every spec.md AC proof (lines 81-93,98) names the exact test present in orphan_gate_test.go / impl_test.go; no mismatch. All discriminating -run clauses are exact-named.
